name: Sendero del Charco Negro – circular from Santa Cruz de la Zarza (PR-TO 12)
name_original: Sendero del Charco Negro – circular desde Santa Cruz de la Zarza (PR-TO 12)
region_intro: Hiking around Santa Cruz De La Zarza offers access to a network of trails through the local countryside. The region features paths that traverse agricultural areas and provide views of the surrounding plains. Routes are generally characterized by moderate elevation changes, making them suitable for various hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Santa Cruz De La Zarza The most popular hiking route is Sendero del Charco Negro – circular from Santa Cruz de la Zarza (PR-TO 12), an 8.5 miles (13.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the local area with varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tarancón - Belinchón - circular via the Negrín Path (PR-CU 116), a difficult 13.9 miles (22.3 km) path. This longer trail offers a more challenging experience through the wider landscape. Local hikers also love the Tarancón loop from Tarancón, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail leading through open countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 45 minutes. Hiking around Santa Cruz De La Zarza is defined by countryside paths, agricultural areas, and local trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Santa Cruz De La Zarza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from 7 reviews. More than 37 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa Cruz De La Zarza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Santa Cruz De La Zarza?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;19 hiking routes&lt;/strong&gt; available around Santa Cruz De La Zarza, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy hiking options in Santa Cruz De La Zarza?
A: Yes, Santa Cruz De La Zarza offers several easy hiking trails. You can find &lt;strong&gt;10 easy routes&lt;/strong&gt;, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19434056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villamanrique de Tajo loop from Villamanrique de Tajo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.4 miles (7.1 km) long, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38172131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Cabeza Gorda loop from Tarancón&lt;/a&gt;, covering 3.4 miles (5.5 km).

Q: What are some longer or more challenging hikes in the area?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013706425/tarancon-belinchon-circular-via-the-negrin-path-pr-cu-116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarancón - Belinchón - circular via the Negrín Path (PR-CU 116)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13.9 miles (22.3 km) route. There are also &lt;strong&gt;8 moderate trails&lt;/strong&gt;, including the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018344745/sendero-del-charco-negro-circular-from-santa-cruz-de-la-zarza-pr-to-12" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Charco Negro – circular from Santa Cruz de la Zarza (PR-TO 12)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.5 miles (13.6 km) long.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Santa Cruz De La Zarza?
A: Yes, many of the trails in the area are circular. Notable circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018344745/sendero-del-charco-negro-circular-from-santa-cruz-de-la-zarza-pr-to-12" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Charco Negro – circular from Santa Cruz de la Zarza (PR-TO 12)&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013706425/tarancon-belinchon-circular-via-the-negrin-path-pr-cu-116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarancón - Belinchón - circular via the Negrín Path (PR-CU 116)&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarancón loop from Tarancón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Santa Cruz De La Zarza?
A: The hiking trails around Santa Cruz De La Zarza primarily feature &lt;strong&gt;countryside paths and agricultural areas&lt;/strong&gt;. You can expect varied terrain with generally moderate elevation changes, offering views of the surrounding plains.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Santa Cruz De La Zarza suitable for families?
A: Many of the &lt;strong&gt;10 easy routes&lt;/strong&gt; in Santa Cruz De La Zarza are suitable for families, especially those looking for shorter walks with gentle inclines. These trails often traverse open countryside, providing a pleasant environment for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the trails in Santa Cruz De La Zarza?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on each trail are not always listed, the general nature of the countryside paths and agricultural areas around Santa Cruz De La Zarza often makes them suitable for walks with dogs. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash and respect local signage.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see near the hiking trails?
A: Near the hiking trails, you can explore several interesting landmarks. These include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4207392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge of Fuentidueña de Tajo&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2780452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villamanrique de Tajo Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villatobas&lt;/a&gt; or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4079883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Most Holy Christ of Humilladero&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Santa Cruz De La Zarza?
A: The trails in Santa Cruz De La Zarza are well-regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of &lt;strong&gt;3.9 stars from 7 reviews&lt;/strong&gt;. Hikers often praise the peaceful countryside and the variety of routes available for different abilities.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes in Santa Cruz De La Zarza?
A: Hikes in Santa Cruz De La Zarza vary in duration. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38172131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Cabeza Gorda loop from Tarancón&lt;/a&gt; can take around 1 hour 30 minutes, while moderate trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018344745/sendero-del-charco-negro-circular-from-santa-cruz-de-la-zarza-pr-to-12" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Charco Negro&lt;/a&gt; typically take about 3 hours 40 minutes. Longer, difficult routes can extend to over 6 hours.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Santa Cruz De La Zarza?
A: Given its location in Spain, the best times for hiking in Santa Cruz De La Zarza are generally during the &lt;strong&gt;spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November)&lt;/strong&gt;. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, making for more comfortable conditions to explore the open countryside and agricultural paths.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: While specific parking information for every trailhead is not detailed, routes often start and end near towns or villages like Santa Cruz De La Zarza or Tarancón, where &lt;strong&gt;public parking is generally available&lt;/strong&gt;. It's advisable to check local maps or komoot tour details for specific starting points.
tips:
Very close to the border with the province of Cuenca, the town of Santa Cruz de la Zarza offers an interesting alternative for those who enjoy visiting a beautiful and quiet town combining nature. A few kilometers away is the marked out itinerary PR-TO 12 known as the Charco Negro path, which runs through a very interesting and picturesque landscape, crossing oak forests of great importance in the area.
In addition, in the town you will have the necessary services to start your route and do not miss visiting the church of Santiago Apóstol next to the attractive building of the town hall.
